name: PB-07 | Critical CVE Detection & Automated Patch Ticketing
description: Production playbook. Ingests vulnerability scanner findings from Tenable/Nessus/OpenVAS, Windows patch status from WSUS/SCCM, and CISA KEV alerts from Wazuh. Enriches CVE data via NVD and CISA KEV API. Assesses host criticality and network exposure. Auto-generates prioritized patch tickets in Jira with SLA deadlines, assigns to the correct team, and creates TheHive tracking cases for actively exploited CVEs.

trigger.description: Listens for Wazuh vulnerability and patch alerts on rule group: vulnerability,pb07 at level >= 11

nodes.name: Webhook Trigger
nodes.description: Entry point. Receives vulnerability scanner or patch management alert payload.

nodes.name: Artifact Extraction & Priority Classification
nodes.description: Normalizes vulnerability alert fields. Pre-classifies ticket priority based on CVSS score, CISA KEV status, and host criticality. Determines SLA deadline based on priority tier.

nodes.name: NVD API — Full CVE Detail Lookup
nodes.description: Queries the NIST National Vulnerability Database API for full CVE details: description, CVSS v3.1 vector breakdown, CWE classification, affected CPE configurations, published date, and last modified date.

nodes.name: CISA KEV API — Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Check
nodes.description: Queries the CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog to confirm if this CVE has been actively exploited in the wild. KEV entries include the required remediation due date set by CISA for US federal agencies — used as a reference SLA even in private sector.

nodes.name: Tenable — Host Asset Details & Vulnerability History
nodes.description: Queries Tenable.io asset API for full host context: operating system, installed software, open ports, vulnerability history, and asset criticality score (ACS). ACS is Tenable's proprietary host importance rating based on network centrality and asset value.

nodes.name: Network Exposure Assessment
nodes.description: Determines whether the vulnerability is exploitable from the internet based on host classification, CVSS attack vector, and open port context. Internet-facing hosts with network-exploitable CVEs represent maximum exposure.

nodes.name: Vulnerability Priority Rating Calculation
nodes.description: Calculates a Vulnerability Priority Rating (VPR) combining CVSS score, CISA KEV status, network exposure, host criticality, and ransomware use flags. Determines final ticket priority and SLA deadline.

nodes.name: Jira — Duplicate Ticket Check
nodes.description: Searches Jira for an existing open ticket for this CVE on this host before creating a new one. Prevents duplicate tickets when the same vulnerability is detected in multiple scan cycles.

params.jql: project = {{ variables.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }} AND summary ~ '{{ $node_02_extract.cve_id }}' AND text ~ '{{ $node_02_extract.affected_hostname }}' AND statusCategory != Done ORDER BY created DESC

nodes.name: Ticket Creation Decision Gate
nodes.description: Creates new ticket if no existing open ticket found. If duplicate exists, updates the existing ticket with new scan data and re-evaluates priority instead of creating duplicate.

nodes.name: Jira — Create Prioritized Patch Ticket
nodes.description: Creates a structured Jira vulnerability remediation ticket with full CVE context, NVD details, CISA KEV status, host exposure assessment, SLA due date, and remediation steps. Assigns to the correct team based on priority.

nodes.name: Jira — Update Existing Ticket
nodes.description: If a duplicate ticket exists, adds a comment with updated scan data and re-evaluates priority. Prevents ticket sprawl while keeping the remediation record current.

nodes.name: TheHive — Create Case for CISA KEV Vulnerabilities
nodes.description: Creates a TheHive incident case only for CISA KEV confirmed CVEs and CVSS 9.0+ vulnerabilities on critical infrastructure. Lower severity findings are tracked in Jira only — TheHive is reserved for vulnerabilities with confirmed active exploitation.

input.title: PB-07 | {{ $node_04_cisa_kev.kev_confirmed == true ? 'CISA KEV' : 'CRITICAL CVE' }} — {{ $node_02_extract.cve_id }} on {{ $node_02_extract.affected_hostname }} | VPR: {{ $node_07_priority_score.vpr_score }}
input.description:
## Actively Exploited Vulnerability — Urgent Remediation Required

**CVE:** {{ $node_02_extract.cve_id }}
**CVSS Score:** {{ $node_02_extract.cvss_score }} / 10
**VPR Score:** {{ $node_07_priority_score.vpr_score }} / 100
**Priority:** {{ $node_07_priority_score.final_priority }}
**SLA Due:** {{ $node_07_priority_score.final_sla_due }}
**Jira Ticket:** {{ $node_10_create_jira.jira_ticket_url | default($node_10b_update_jira.jira_ticket_url) }}

### CISA KEV Status
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| KEV Confirmed | {{ $node_04_cisa_kev.kev_confirmed }} |
| Vendor/Product | {{ $node_04_cisa_kev.kev_vendor_project }} — {{ $node_04_cisa_kev.kev_product }} |
| Short Description | {{ $node_04_cisa_kev.kev_short_description }} |
| Required Action | {{ $node_04_cisa_kev.kev_required_action }} |
| CISA Due Date | {{ $node_04_cisa_kev.kev_due_date }} |
| Ransomware Use | {{ $node_04_cisa_kev.kev_ransomware_use }} |

### Affected System
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Hostname | {{ $node_02_extract.affected_hostname }} |
| IP | {{ $node_02_extract.affected_ip }} |
| OS | {{ $node_02_extract.affected_os }} |
| Software | {{ $node_02_extract.affected_software }} {{ $node_02_extract.affected_version }} |
| Host Classification | {{ $node_02_extract.host_classification }} |
| Exposure Level | {{ $node_06_exposure_check.exposure_level }} |
| Internet Facing | {{ $node_06_exposure_check.is_internet_facing }} |
| Tenable ACR | {{ $node_05_host_context.asset_criticality_score }} |
| Open Vulnerabilities | Critical: {{ $node_05_host_context.asset_vuln_count_critical }} · High: {{ $node_05_host_context.asset_vuln_count_high }} |

### CVE Technical Details
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Description | {{ $node_03_nvd_lookup.nvd_description }} |
| CVSS v3.1 Vector | {{ $node_03_nvd_lookup.nvd_cvss_v3_vector }} |
| Attack Vector | {{ $node_03_nvd_lookup.nvd_attack_vector }} |
| Attack Complexity | {{ $node_03_nvd_lookup.nvd_attack_complexity }} |
| Privileges Required | {{ $node_03_nvd_lookup.nvd_privileges_required }} |
| CWE | {{ $node_03_nvd_lookup.nvd_cwe }} |

### Remediation Checklist
- [ ] Verify Jira patch ticket created and assigned: {{ $node_10_create_jira.jira_ticket_url | default($node_10b_update_jira.jira_ticket_url) }}
- [ ] Confirm patch exists and is available from vendor
- [ ] Test patch in staging environment before production
- [ ] Assess if temporary mitigations can reduce exposure while patch is applied (WAF rule, network ACL, service disable)
- [ ] Schedule emergency maintenance window if CISA KEV confirmed
- [ ] Scan all hosts running {{ $node_02_extract.affected_software }} {{ $node_02_extract.affected_version }} for the same CVE
- [ ] Verify patch applied — rescan host in Tenable post-remediation
- [ ] Close Jira ticket and this case with remediation evidence

nodes.name: Slack — Vulnerability Alert
nodes.description: Posts structured vulnerability alert to the vuln management channel. CISA KEV and critical exposure vulnerabilities get elevated color and messaging.

payload.text: *PB-07 | {{ $node_04_cisa_kev.kev_confirmed == true ? ':rotating_light: CISA KEV — ACTIVELY EXPLOITED' : 'Vulnerability Alert' }}*

metrics.estimated_mttd_reduction: Real-time — scanner findings streamed immediately vs. weekly report review
metrics.estimated_mttr_reduction: Patch ticket created with full context in < 10 seconds vs. 30-60 min manual triage
metrics.duplicate_prevention: Jira search before creation prevents ticket sprawl across scan cycles
metrics.kev_sla: CISA KEV CVEs get 3-day SLA vs. standard 7-day critical — aligned with federal remediation mandate
